support_note: Day-of-Surgery variant is the canonical setting for OR utilization and capacity planning — it covers admission-through-PACU which is where utilization is measured.
Day-of-Surgery variant supports elective/emergency mix analysis since both streams flow through the day-of-surgery envelope.
Day-of-Surgery variant is the natural setting for PACU bottleneck analysis — PACU is its terminal phase.
Day-of-Surgery variant supports parallel induction analysis since induction occurs within its intraoperative phase.
Full Perioperative variant supports OR utilization at a more comprehensive level, including pre-op clinic coordination and downstream ward capacity.
Full Perioperative variant supports elective/emergency mix across the entire service line including pre-op clinic scheduling interactions.
Full Perioperative variant captures PACU bottlenecks together with downstream ward bed availability — PACU congestion often cascades from ward bed shortages.
Full Perioperative variant can include parallel induction but at this scope the optimization's impact is diluted by longer pre-op and post-op phases.
Orthopedic OR Ergonomics variant focuses on intraoperative team movement rather than parallel induction, but shares the underlying concern of intraoperative workflow optimization.
